Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BOOKER T. FOGGY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel First Amerian Title To Produce Documents Per The Subpoena Duces Tecum Served On That Company And Motion To Authorize Recording Of Notice Of Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Joan Lynn Foggy's Motion to Compel First American Title to Produce Documents Per the Subpoena Duces Tecum Served on That Company and Motion to Authorize Recording of Notice of Lis Pendens. The motion is DENIED. Decedent Booker T. Foggy died with an ownership interest in two pieces of real property: 1820 Alabama Street and 1838 Alabama Street, San Francisco. Bruce Foggy was appointed Administrator on October 4, 2018. Movant Joan Foggy recorded a Notice of Lis Pendens as to 1838 Alabama Street, through her then-attorney Roger Meredith, on January 10, 2020. On Jun 18, 2020, the Administrator filed Ex Parte Application for Authority to Execute Exclusive Listing Agreement as to both properties. Joan and her attorney were served notice. The ex parte petition was unopposed, and Order Approving Ex Parte Application was entered September 9, 2020. A Notice of Withdrawal of the lis pendens was filed by Meredith on November 2, 2020, while still Joan's attorney. On November 12, 2020, the Administrator filed Notice of Sale of Real Property at Private Sale, as to both properties. Joan and her attorney were served notice. The Administrator published Notice of Sale of both properties and filed Proof of Publication on November 19, 2020. On January 5, 2021, the Administrator filed a Report of Sale and Petition for Order Confirming Sale of Real Property for each of the Alabama Street properties. A hearing was held on the Petition on April 5, 2021, and neither movant nor her counsel appeared or objected. Following the hearing, Orders Confirming Sale of Real Property for each of the Alabama Street properties were entered on April 13, 2021, more than 19 months ago. On May 5, 2022, Meredith withdrew as counsel for Joan. On July 15, 2022, more than a year after the orders confirming sale were entered, movant filed Objection to the Sale of Estate Real Property at 1820-1838 Alabama Street. The court held a regularly noticed hearing on September 9, 2022 on the Administrator's Petition to Approve Accounting and for Final Distribution and Fees. Joan appeared at the hearing and after discussion, the court directed her to file any written opposition to the Petition for Final Distribution no later than September 26, 2022. The hearing was continued to October 10, 2022. Joan did not file an objection to the Petition and did not appear at the October 10, 2022 hearing. On October 28, 2022, the court entered Order for Final Distribution. The motion now before the court was filed October 7, 2022. Joan Lynn Foggy's Motion to Compel First American Title to Produce Documents Per the Subpoena Duces Tecum Served on That Company and Motion to Authorize Recording of Notice of Lis Pendens is DENIED. As a threshold issue, movant did not comply with Local Rule 14.10 in filing this motion on the Probate Department's law and motion hearing calendar. [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BOOKER T. FOGGY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Real Estate Sale Of Estate Real Property And Motion To Set Asdie Order To Authorize The Private Sale Of Estate Real Property
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Joan Lynn Foggy's Motion to Set Aside Real Estate Sale of Estate Real Property and Motion to Set Aside Order to Authorize the Private Sale of Real Property [CCP section 473(b)]. The motion is DENIED. Decedent Booker T. Foggy died with an ownership interest in two pieces of real property: 1820 Alabama Street and 1838 Alabama Street, San Francisco. Bruce Foggy was appointed Administrator on October 4, 2018. Movant Joan Foggy recorded a Notice of Lis Pendens as to 1838 Alabama Street, through her then-attorney Roger Meredith, on January 10, 2020. On Jun 18, 2020, the Administrator filed Ex Parte Application for Authority to Execute Exclusive Listing Agreement as to both properties. Joan and her attorney were served notice. The ex parte petition was unopposed, and Order Approving Ex Parte Application was entered September 9, 2020. A Notice of Withdrawal of the lis pendens was filed by Meredith on November 2, 2020, while still Joan's attorney. On November 12, 2020, the Administrator filed Notice of Sale of Real Property at Private Sale, as to both properties. Joan and her attorney were served notice. The Administrator published Notice of Sale of both properties and filed Proof of Publication on November 19, 2020. On January 5, 2021, the Administrator filed a Report of Sale and Petition for Order Confirming Sale of Real Property for each of the Alabama Street properties. A hearing was held on the Petition on April 5, 2021, and neither movant nor her counsel appeared or objected. Following the hearing, Orders Confirming Sale of Real Property for each of the Alabama Street properties were entered on April 13, 2021, more than 19 months ago. On May 5, 2022, Meredith withdrew as counsel for Joan. On July 15, 2022, more than a year after the orders confirming sale were entered, movant filed Objection to the Sale of Estate Real Property at 1820-1838 Alabama Street. The court held a regularly noticed hearing on September 9, 2022 on the Administrator's Petition to Approve Accounting and for Final Distribution and Fees. Joan appeared at the hearing and after discussion, the court directed her to file any written opposition to the Petition for Final Distribution no later than September 26, 2022. The hearing was continued to October 10, 2022. Joan did not file an objection to the Petition and did not appear at the October 10, 2022 hearing. On October 28, 2022, the court entered Order for Final Distribution. The motion now before the court was filed October 7, 2022. The Motion to Set Aside Real Estate Sale of Estate Real Property and Motion to Set Aside Order to Authorize the Private Sale of Real Property [CCP section 473(b)] is DENIED. This motion seeks relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b), asserting that the moving party's excusable neglect, inadvertence, or surprise at these proceedings, warrants the court setting aside orders authorizing sale of the real properties, 1820 Alabama Street and 1838 Alabama Street. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b), The court may, upon any terms as may be just, relieve a party or his or her legal representative from a judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against him or her through his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Application for this relief shall be accompanied by a copy of the answer or other pleading proposed to be filed therein, otherwise the application shall not be granted, and shall be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken. [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BOOKER T. FOGGY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Set Aside Real Estate Sale Of Estate Real Property And Motion To Set Asdie Order To Authorize The Private Sale Of Estate Real Property (Added For The Purpose Of Posting Tenatative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] The statute requires that this motion be denied on at multiple grounds: First, there is no "copy of the answer or other pleading proposed to be filed therein" accompanying this motion, and thus, "the application shall not be granted." Further, even if this defect were cured, the motion is untimely under the statute and cannot be granted, because it was filed more than six months after the Orders Confirming Sale of Real Property for each of the Alabama Street properties were entered, on April 13, 2021 ("[the application] shall be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken."). In addition, Code of Civil Procedure section 473.5 is not applicable, here, as (1) there was no "default judgment" entered against any party; (2) a Report of Sale and Petition for Order Confirming Sale of Real Property is not a "complaint" and does not require personal service of summons; (3) there is a contested showing that Joan and her then-attorney were served notice of Report of Sale and Petition for Order Confirming Sale of Real Property or related proceeding. (See Dankbar Decl. at para. 2, 5, 6, 7 and related exhibits). As with the request to set aside the orders under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b), movant has not complied with section 473.5's requirement to submit a proposed answer or response to the Petition, or otherwise made a showing by which the court can determine the existence of a "meritorious defense." Code of Civil Procedure section 86 does not apply here, as that statute applies specifically to "limited civil cases" and these proceedings are not a "limited civil case." Finally, movant cites Code of Civil Procedure section 128, the powers of the court, to argue that the orders should be put aside. On the record before the court, it has "control[ed] its process and orders so as to make them conform to law and justice." (Code of Civil Procedure section 128(a)(8)). Administrator Bruce Foggy is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BOOKER T. FOGGY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel First Amerian Title To Produce Documents Per The Subpoena Duces Tecum Served On That Company And Motion To Authorize Recording Of Notice Of Lis Pendens (Added For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] The request to compel First American Title to respond to the subpoena dated July 12, 2022 is procedurally defective, and must be denied on those grounds. First American was not served notice of this motion (see, Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.480; Rules of Court, rule 3.1346). Even if the lack of proper notice was corrected or waived, the statutory time to seek this relief has expired. First American filed written objections to this subpoena with the court on July 26, 2022, and served all parties, including Joan and her then-attorney. Serving such written objections automatically excuses the subpoenaed custodian of records from producing the subpoenaed records until the court orders their production (Code of Civil Procedure section 1985.3(g)). The subpoenaing party must then bring a motion to compel within 60 days after the date the objections are served. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.480(b); see Unzipped Apparel, LLC v. Bader (2007) 156 CA4th 123, 127; Board of Registered Nursing v. Sup.Ct. (Johnson & Johnson) (2021) 59 CA5th 1011, 1032-1033.) 60 days from July 26, 2022 was September 24, 2022. The motion was filed outside that time period on October 7, 2022. An additional fatal flaw in the requested relief is that the underlying subpoena also does not appear to comply with notice and specificity requirements under CCP sections 1985.3 and 2020.410. The request for authority to re-record the Notice of Lis Pendens recorded on January 20, 2020, or to record a new lis pendens, is denied as moot. Orders Confirming Sale of Real Property for each of the Alabama Street properties were entered more than 18 months ago on April 13, 2021. An Order for Final Distribution was entered on October 28, 2022. Administration of this estate has been completed. A "lis pendens" is a recorded document giving constructive notice that an action has been filed affecting title to or right to possession of the real property described in the notice. There is no action before this court affecting to or right to possession of either of the Alabama Street properties, and thus no basis on which to record a notice of lis pendens. Administrator Bruce Foggy is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]
